uplink._note: Upstream TTN example INPUT reused; expected.data produced by the ORACLE method (running reference/upstream-codec.js on this input) then normalized: battery (already volts) -> battery; temperature -> air.temperature; humidity -> air.relativeHumidity; status flags -> camelCase extra status.

uplink.description: Mid-range climate, status 0, minimum battery level (2.5 V)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; expected.data from the ORACLE method (reference/upstream-codec.js) then normalized. battery level nibble 0 -> 2.5 V.

uplink.description: High temperature & humidity, status 2, maximum battery (4.0 V)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; expected.data from the ORACLE method (reference/upstream-codec.js) then normalized. battery level nibble 15 -> 4.0 V; trailing zero bytes are ignored by the fixed 4-byte layout.

uplink.description: Below-freezing temperature (-12 C), status 3, battery 2.7 V
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds input; expected.data from the ORACLE method (reference/upstream-codec.js) then normalized. temperature = (byte2 & 0x7f) - 32 = 20 - 32 = -12 C exercises the freezing-point offset.
Upstream returns a bare {} here; this module's contract forbids that, so we report an error instead.
